title: GEO: Generative Engine Optimization
core_claim: Formalizes GEO and GEO-Bench; reports visibility gains from content changes such as statistics, citations, and fluency.
geo_relevance: Direct origin of the field
risk_notes: Generation-stage evaluation; simplified candidate-source setting; tactics can be copied without measuring utility.

title: C-SEO Bench: Does Conversational SEO Work?
core_claim: Evaluates C-SEO across tasks, domains, and multiple adopting actors; finds many current methods weak or harmful while retrieval/context rank remains important.
method_or_benchmark: Benchmark for citation ranking and multi-actor adoption
geo_relevance: Critical adjacent evidence for GEO limits
risk_notes: Negative result may conflict with vendor narratives; very important for avoiding over-optimization claims.

title: What Generative Search Engines Like and How to Optimize Web Content Cooperatively
core_claim: Introduces AutoGEO: learns engine preference rules, rewrites documents, and balances visibility with generative engine utility.
method_or_benchmark: Preference-rule extraction; AutoGEO_API; AutoGEO_Mini with RL
geo_relevance: Direct next step after GEO-Bench
risk_notes: May overfit engine/domain rules; full-pipeline retrieval effects must be separately tested.

title: From Experience to Skill: Multi-Agent Generative Engine Optimization via Reusable Strategy Learning
core_claim: Reframes GEO as reusable strategy learning; introduces MAGEO, Twin Branch Evaluation Protocol, DSV-CF metric, and MSME-GEO-Bench.
method_or_benchmark: Multi-agent planning, editing, fidelity-aware evaluation, hierarchical memory, and strategy distillation
geo_relevance: Core contribution to multi-agent trustworthy GEO
risk_notes: New benchmark and metrics need independent reproduction; code appears research-grade and scoped to core method.

title: CC-GSEO-Bench: A Content-Centric Benchmark for Measuring Source Influence in Generative Search Engines
core_claim: Moves beyond exposure to source influence: Exposure, Faithful Credit, Causal Impact plus document quality dimensions.
geo_relevance: Important metric upgrade for GEO
risk_notes: LLM-judge design and offline simulation require validation against live engines.

title: Role-Augmented Intent-Driven Generative Search Engine Optimization
core_claim: Models search intent through role-augmented reflective refinement and extends GEO-style evaluation with query variation.
geo_relevance: Relevant to query-intent expansion and content planning
risk_notes: Likely overlaps with prompt-based rewriting and intent clustering; needs stronger comparison to AutoGEO/C-SEO.

title: Generative Engine Optimization: How to Dominate AI Search
core_claim: Compares AI search and Google across verticals, languages, and paraphrases; emphasizes earned media, scannability, and engine-specific strategy.
geo_relevance: Useful practitioner bridge; less methodologically central than benchmark papers
risk_notes: Strategic framing may overgeneralize; verify experimental details before product decisions.

title: SAGEO Arena: A Realistic Environment for Evaluating Search-Augmented Generative Engine Optimization
core_claim: Argues that existing GEO benchmarks abstract away retrieval and reranking; proposes realistic stage-level analysis over search-augmented generative engines.
geo_relevance: Very important because GEO failures often happen before generation
risk_notes: Work in progress; code availability not confirmed in this pass.

title: Diagnosing and Repairing Citation Failures in Generative Engine Optimization
core_claim: Introduces citation failure taxonomy and AgentGEO diagnostic repair workflow; targets citation rather than generic contribution.
geo_relevance: Directly addresses attribution and traffic-driving citation
risk_notes: Agent loop can be costly; failure taxonomy should be tested across engines and languages.

title: AgenticGEO: A Self-Evolving Agentic System for Generative Engine Optimization
core_claim: Uses MAP-Elites strategy archive and co-evolving critic for content-conditioned strategy selection and multi-turn rewriting.
geo_relevance: Directly relevant to adaptive GEO automation
risk_notes: May optimize against proxy feedback; needs robust live-engine and full-pipeline validation.

title: Beyond Retrieval: Modeling Confidence Decay and Deterministic Agentic Platforms in Generative Engine Optimization
core_claim: Proposes moving from probabilistic RAG-centric GEO toward deterministic multi-agent intent routing and agent handoff.
geo_relevance: Adjacent future-looking architecture
risk_notes: Speculative and product-specific; treat as idea source, not established evidence.

title: GEM-Bench: A Benchmark for Ad-Injected Response Generation within Generative Engine Marketing
core_claim: Benchmarks ad-injected response generation in chatbot and search scenarios with user satisfaction and engagement metrics.
geo_relevance: Adjacent monetization layer for GEO and AI search
risk_notes: Advertising can degrade trust; separate organic GEO from paid insertion.

title: Adversarial Search Engine Optimization for Large Language Models
core_claim: Shows website/plugin content can manipulate LLM selections and create a prisoner's-dilemma dynamic among competitors.
geo_relevance: Essential safety boundary for GEO
risk_notes: Do not operationalize attack tactics; use for defense and policy design.

title: Ranking Manipulation for Conversational Search Engines
core_claim: Studies prompt-injection-style ranking manipulation in conversational search and transfer to production-like systems.
method_or_benchmark: Adversarial product ranking dataset and attacks
geo_relevance: Security-critical adjacent work
risk_notes: Attack details should be handled carefully; relevant for robustness benchmarks.

title: GASLITEing the Retrieval: Exploring Vulnerabilities in Dense Embedding-based Search
core_claim: Shows dense retrieval can be vulnerable to adversarial passages and very low poisoning rates under concept-targeted attacks.
method_or_benchmark: Adversarial passage generation for dense retrieval
geo_relevance: Important for retrieval-stage GEO risk
risk_notes: Focus on defense; validates that retrieval optimization can become attack surface.

title: Dynamics of Adversarial Attacks on Large Language Model-Based Search Engines
core_claim: Models ranking manipulation as an infinitely repeated prisoner's dilemma and analyzes when cooperation or attacks emerge.
geo_relevance: Useful for governance and market design
risk_notes: Theoretical assumptions need empirical grounding.

title: What Evidence Do Language Models Find Convincing?
core_claim: Builds ConflictingQA and finds models can over-weight query relevance while under-weighting evidence features humans value.
geo_relevance: Explains why citation quality and corpus quality matter for GEO
risk_notes: Not GEO-specific, but highly relevant to source selection behavior.

title: ConflictBank: A Benchmark for Evaluating the Influence of Knowledge Conflicts in LLM
core_claim: Creates large-scale claim-evidence and QA pairs to evaluate conflicts among retrieved, encoded, and interacting knowledge.
geo_relevance: Useful for GEO where fresh web content conflicts with model prior knowledge
risk_notes: Broad LLM reliability work, not GEO-specific.

title: When Search Engine Services meet Large Language Models: Visions and Challenges
core_claim: Surveys mutual integration of search engines and LLMs, including Search4LLM and LLM4Search architectures and challenges.
geo_relevance: Background map for where GEO hooks into search systems
risk_notes: Survey-level; use for architecture vocabulary, not GEO claims.

title: E-GEO: A Testbed for Generative Engine Optimization in E-Commerce
core_claim: Introduces an e-commerce-specific GEO testbed with product-query settings and evaluates rewriting heuristics for AI-shopping visibility.
method_or_benchmark: Prompt/meta-optimization over e-commerce product descriptions and ranking outcomes
geo_relevance: Core vertical benchmark for commerce-oriented GEO
risk_notes: Needs independent reproduction and comparison to AutoGEO/C-SEO; several downstream repos cite it with stronger claims than the paper alone may justify.

title: BRIGHT: A Realistic and Challenging Benchmark for Reasoning-Intensive Retrieval
core_claim: Provides a challenging retrieval benchmark where queries require reasoning, relevant to retrieval-stage evaluation in AI search.
geo_relevance: Background for full-pipeline GEO retrieval testing
risk_notes: Not GEO-specific; useful for retrieval stress tests only.

title: Large Language Models for Information Retrieval: A Survey
core_claim: Surveys LLM use across query understanding, retrieval, ranking, and evaluation.
geo_relevance: Background architecture and vocabulary for GEO retrieval/ranking layers
risk_notes: Older than the GEO wave; use as IR background, not GEO evidence.

title: Search-o1: Agentic Search-Enhanced Large Reasoning Models
core_claim: Combines agentic search with LLM reasoning for complex information needs, useful for understanding search-augmented answer generation.
geo_relevance: Background for search-augmented generative engines and agentic retrieval loops
risk_notes: Not about content optimization; relevant to engine architecture only.

title: Is ChatGPT Good at Search? Investigating Large Language Models as Re-Ranking Agents
core_claim: Studies ChatGPT as a search reranking agent and highlights LLM-based ranking behavior.
geo_relevance: Background for GEO reranking-stage reasoning
risk_notes: Pre-GEO and not production-engine-specific; use as reranking context.
